message:
feat(cli): add setup self-heal fingerprint cache and optimize preflight stats

Setup self-heal:
- Replace the full handle_setup() call in run_setup_self_heal_for_server
  with an inline implementation that caches the setup state to a
  per-project JSON file under .setup-self-heal/. The cache records the
  project dir, server URL, token fingerprint (FNV-1a hash, not raw
  secret), target agent list, and file fingerprints (existence, size,
  mtime) of all config files that setup would write.
- On subsequent server starts, skip setup entirely when the static
  parameters match and all target config files have unchanged
  fingerprints. If static params match but files changed, run
  check_status() first and skip the full setup run when all files
  already have correct server entries.
- Load target agents from the previous cache when available to avoid
  re-running agent detection on every startup.
- Skip project identity resolution (which may hit the filesystem) when
  hooks are disabled.

Preflight banner stats:
- Add query_preflight_banner_stats_from_sqlite_sequence() as a fast
  path that reads approximate row counts from sqlite_sequence instead
  of running COUNT(*) on all 5 tables. Falls back to MAX(id) when
  sqlite_sequence is unavailable.
- Add test: query_preflight_banner_stats_batched_uses_max_id_fast_path.

Logging:
- Change default_release_log_filter() from "info" (all crates) to
  "warn" globally with explicit "info" for the 6 mcp_agent_mail_*
  crates. This suppresses noisy third-party crate logs while keeping
  agent mail diagnostics at info level.
- When suppress_runtime_logs_for_tui is true, apply the "off" filter
  before checking AM_ALLOW_DEBUG_STARTUP_LOGS so TUI mode never emits
  tracing lines that corrupt alternate-screen rendering.

Core setup:
- Refactor save_token_to_env_file() to short-circuit when the existing
  file content would be unchanged, avoiding unnecessary writes.

Formatting: pattern_overlap.rs, reservations.rs line-break fixes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
